paper_title: Late Holocene dietary and cultural variability on the Xingu River, Amazon Basin: A stable isotopic approach

methods.description: Statistical procedures used to analyze isotopic datasets for normality and significance of variation between dietary groups and taxa.

steps.description: All isotopic datasets were tested for normality using the Shapiro Wilk test.
steps.searchable_summary: perform Shapiro Wilk test for normality on isotopic datasets

steps.description: The significance of the variation of δ 15 N and δ 18 O between dietary groups was determined using comparative ANOVA tests.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct ANOVA tests to determine significance of δ 15 N and δ 18 O variation

steps.description: ANOVA test was applied to compare δ 15 N values with previous studies.
steps.searchable_summary: apply ANOVA to compare δ 15 N values with historical data

steps.description: Post-hoc Tukey pair-wise comparison test was used where variance was significant.
steps.searchable_summary: perform Tukey pair-wise comparison for significant ANOVA results

steps.description: Kruskal-Wallis tests were used to determine significance of δ 13 C co and δ 13 C ap variation.
steps.searchable_summary: use Kruskal-Wallis test for δ 13 C co and δ 13 C ap variation analysis

steps.description: Pairwise comparisons using Wilcoxon comparisons were conducted.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct Wilcoxon pairwise comparisons for δ 13 C data

methods.description: Procedures for extracting and analyzing bone collagen and tooth enamel for stable isotope analysis to reconstruct past diets.

steps.description: Bone collagen was extracted from skeletal elements following standard procedures.
steps.searchable_summary: extract bone collagen using standard demineralization and gelatinization procedures

steps.description: Approximately 1.5 grams of bone was demineralized in 0.5M HCl at 4˚C.
Sample was rinsed in deionized water and gelatinized in pH3 HCl at 70˚C.
steps.searchable_summary: gelatinize demineralized bone in pH3 HCl

steps.description: Solution was filtered, frozen, and lyophilized.
steps.searchable_summary: filter, freeze, and lyophilize gelatinized bone solution

steps.description: 1mg of extracted collagen was analyzed for δ 13 C and δ 15 N using mass spectrometry.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze collagen for δ 13 C and δ 15 N using mass spectrometry
outputs: δ 13 C and δ 15 N data

steps.description: Teeth were cleaned and enamel powder was obtained using a diamond-tipped drill.
steps.searchable_summary: obtain enamel powder from teeth using diamond-tipped drill

steps.description: Enamel powder was pre-treated to remove contaminants.
steps.searchable_summary: pre-treat enamel powder to remove contaminants

steps.description: Treated enamel was analyzed for δ 13 C using mass spectrometry.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze treated enamel for δ 13 C using mass spectrometry
